sections.title: Classification of AI Systems as High-Risk

articles.title: Classification Rules for High-Risk AI Systems
articles.summary: Two classification routes: (1) safety component of product under Annex I legislation; (2) falls within Annex III use-case areas. Exemptions for systems not posing significant risk.

articles.title: Amendments to Annex III
articles.summary: Commission may amend Annex III by delegated acts to add/modify high-risk categories based on severity, frequency, and scale criteria.

articles.title: Compliance with Requirements
articles.summary: High-risk AI systems shall comply with all Section 2 requirements (Articles 9-15), taking into account intended purpose and state of the art.

articles.title: Risk Management System
articles.summary: Continuous iterative risk management system throughout lifecycle: risk identification, estimation, evaluation, and adoption of management measures.

articles.title: Data and Data Governance
articles.summary: Training/validation/testing datasets must meet quality criteria: relevance, representativeness, accuracy, completeness. Data governance including bias examination.

articles.title: Technical Documentation
articles.summary: Technical documentation per Annex IV before market placement, kept up to date, demonstrating Section 2 compliance.

articles.title: Record-Keeping
articles.summary: Automatic recording of events (logs) for risk identification, post-market monitoring, and traceability.

articles.title: Transparency and Provision of Information to Deployers
articles.summary: Systems designed to enable deployers to interpret output. Instructions for use must accompany system with clear, complete information.

articles.title: Human Oversight
articles.summary: Effective human oversight during use: understanding capacities/limitations, monitoring operation, interpreting output, ability to override/interrupt.

articles.title: Accuracy, Robustness and Cybersecurity
articles.summary: Appropriate levels of accuracy, robustness, and cybersecurity. Resilient against errors, faults, and adversarial attempts.

articles.title: Obligations of Providers
articles.summary: Ensure compliance, establish QMS, maintain documentation, keep logs, take corrective actions, affix CE marking.

articles.title: Quality Management System
articles.summary: QMS including regulatory compliance strategy, design techniques, testing procedures, data management, risk management, post-market monitoring.

articles.title: Documentation Keeping
articles.summary: Keep all documentation for at least 10 years after placing on market.

articles.title: Automatically Generated Logs
articles.summary: Keep automatically generated logs for at least 6 months.

articles.title: Corrective Actions
articles.summary: Immediate corrective actions and notification to supply chain if non-compliant.

articles.title: Cooperation with Authorities
articles.summary: Provide information, documentation, and log access on reasoned request.

articles.title: Authorised Representatives
articles.summary: Non-EU providers must appoint EU authorised representative.

articles.title: Obligations of Importers
articles.summary: Verify conformity assessment, documentation, CE marking, and authorised representative.

articles.title: Obligations of Distributors
articles.summary: Verify CE marking, declaration of conformity, and documentation before distribution.

articles.title: Responsibilities Along Value Chain
articles.summary: Substantial modifiers or rebranders become providers.

articles.title: Obligations of Deployers
articles.summary: Use per instructions, ensure human oversight, monitor, keep logs, conduct fundamental rights impact assessment, inform affected persons.

articles.title: Fundamental Rights Impact Assessment
articles.summary: Public bodies and certain private deployers assess impact on fundamental rights before deployment.

sections.title: Notifying Authorities and Notified Bodies
sections.articleSummary: Articles 28-39 establish requirements for notifying authorities and notified bodies: designation, application, requirements, presumption of conformity, subsidiaries, operational obligations, identification, changes, competence challenges, coordination, and third-country bodies.

sections.title: Standards, Conformity Assessment, Certificates, Registration
sections.articleSummary: Articles 40-49 cover harmonised standards, common specifications, presumption of conformity, conformity assessment procedures (internal control or third-party), certificates, derogations, EU declaration of conformity, CE marking, and EU database registration.
